title: Canal+ 'blacklist' threat fuels tensions in the French film industry

title: Why the Ebola surge in the DRC is alarming international health authorities
subhed: The Democratic Republic of the Congo is experiencing a new outbreak of the Ebola virus, which causes a deadly hemorrhagic fever. More than 100 deaths have already been reported, mainly in Ituri Province in the northeast of the country. The World Health Organization has declared a Public Health Emergency of International Concern.

title: Michelin guide transforms itself to become global benchmark for the art of living
subhed: In response to competition from digital platforms, the restaurant guide has begun a major shift, expanding both its scope of expertise and its geographic influence.

title: How Le Pen's party has shifted its economic platform over 15 years
subhed: Ever since Marine Le Pen took control of the far-right Rassemblement National in 2011, it has struggled to present a clear economic platform, to the point of muddying its political identity and sparking questions about its plans.

Gaza's vanished past lives on through photographs accidentally rediscovered in Marseille
subhed: Kegham Djeghalian, a photographer and Armenian genocide survivor, settled in Gaza in 1944. He documented the Palestinian territory through portraits and reports until he died in 1981. His grandson has chosen to exhibit his images of ordinary and joyful moments in Gaza, now devastated by two years of war.

title: Captured, imprisoned and tortured: Russia 'ignores all laws and does whatever it wants' to Ukrainian civilians
subhed: Since the 2022 invasion, Moscow has been arresting citizens from the occupied territories en masse in an effort to crush all resistance. More than 16,000 people are currently imprisoned, according to the Ukrainian Parliament Commissioner for Human Rights.
